steps.title: Use the contrast with Kuroda
steps.content: The sentence compares Kuroda Renji, who used European oil-on-canvas methods, with Hishida Masaru, whose work is described as Nihonga, which is traditional Japanese painting, and then cites his use of silk-scroll pigments as an example.

steps.title: Select the verb that means adopted
steps.content: Choice A implies skepticism, which conflicts with the example. Choice C means releasing or freeing, not shown in the text. Choice D means ignoring traditional methods. Choice B means adopted and follows naturally from the traditional technique described. The answer is B.
